hooks.command: cd ~/theVault && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "auto: $(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H:%M:%S)" > /dev/null 2>&1

INPUT=$(cat); FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | grep -o '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"\([^"]*\)"$/\1/'); case "$FILE_PATH" in *memory/*|*SHARED_CONTEXT*|*DesktopClaudeCode/*|*CLAUDE.md) bash ~/theVault/System/Scripts/sync_session_state.sh 'hook:PostToolUse' 2>/dev/null ;; esac

hooks.prompt:
You are a memory-update agent running after a Claude Code session turn in ~/theVault.

Check for recent commits:
  cd ~/theVault && git log --oneline --since='10 minutes ago'

If there are NO recent commits, output exactly: NO_COMMITS and stop.

If there ARE recent commits:
1. Read the commit messages to understand what was completed.
2. Read /Users/ericmanchester/.claude/projects/-Users-ericmanchester-theVault/memory/project_priorities_2026_03.md
3. If any completed work maps to a listed priority item that is NOT already marked ✅, update that file to mark it ✅ with the date (format: ✅ YYYY-MM-DD).
4. Append a one-line entry to /Users/ericmanchester/theVault/.agents/SHARED_CONTEXT.md under '## Active Work' describing what was completed and when (format: | CLI Sonnet | <what was done> | <files touched> | <date> |).
5. Only edit files if something actually changed — do not make trivial edits.
